data.title: verify: revalidate tool- and API-facing skills after move into skills/
data.description:
## Problem

Several moved skills make claims about current CLIs, APIs, hook behavior, or agent workflows. Before these become canonical shared skills, they should be re-verified against the current environment and source docs.

Initial candidate set:

* `skogai-argc`
* `skogai-git`
* `skogai-gita`
* `skogai-hook-debugger`
* `skogai-mcp-builder`
* `skogai-gptme`
* `skogai-prompting`
* `skogai-planning-with-files`

## Scope

* classify each candidate by dependency type: CLI, framework, integration, API/service, or pure process
* use the existing verify-skill workflow as a starting point, but run it against repo-local `skills/`
* produce a freshness report per reviewed skill and either fix issues or spawn narrower follow-up tickets
* separate structural cleanup from truth-verification so reviewers know whether a skill is wrong, stale, or just messy

## Done when

* each external-facing candidate has a freshness decision: current, needs update, or split out into follow-up work
* obviously stale commands/flags/paths are corrected or documented
* pure-process skills are explicitly identified so they are not over-audited as if they were API guides
* the repo has a repeatable review path for future imported skills with external dependencies
